phase1.output: (Window appears with VBox, HBox, and nested layouts)
phase1.explanation:
VBox and HBox are the most common layouts:

VBox (Vertical Box):
• Arranges nodes vertically (top to bottom)
• setSpacing(10) — space between nodes
• setPadding(new Insets(10)) — padding around edges
• setAlignment(Pos.CENTER) — alignment

HBox (Horizontal Box):
• Arranges nodes horizontally (left to right)
• Same methods as VBox
• Great for button bars

Alignment options:
• Pos.CENTER — center alignment
• Pos.TOP_LEFT — top-left
• Pos.BOTTOM_RIGHT — bottom-right
• Pos.CENTER_LEFT — center-left

Insets:
• new Insets(10) — 10px on all sides
• new Insets(10, 20, 10, 20) — top, right, bottom, left

Nested layouts:
• Layouts inside other layouts
• VBox contains HBox for button rows
• Enables complex UIs

Padding vs Spacing:
• Padding: space between edge and content
• Spacing: space between nodes

phase2.errorMessage: (Window appears with NOTHING inside — empty window!)
phase2.explanation:
You forgot to add children to the VBox!

Without vbox.getChildren().addAll(), the VBox is empty, and the window is blank.

FIX: Add children:
vbox.getChildren().addAll(btn1, btn2, btn3);

Also consider:
• No spacing means nodes are touching
• Add setSpacing() for better appearance

Always verify that you've added your nodes to the layout!

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program that:
1. Creates a VBox with spacing 15 and padding 20
2. Adds three Labels: "Item 1", "Item 2", "Item 3"
3. Creates an HBox with spacing 10
4. Adds three Buttons: "Add", "Edit", "Delete"
5. Nests the HBox inside the VBox (below the labels)
6. Centers everything
7. Shows the window with title "Layout Practice"
dialogueHints: VBox vbox = new VBox(15); vbox.setPadding(new Insets(20));
